audits.body:
Cloning https://aur.archlinux.org/nextcloud-client-appimage-daily.git to /home/mgalgs/aur-sleuth-data/bulk-reports/qwen-qwen3-235b-a22b-2507/aur-sleuth-zid0zwxg...

Performing initial audit of PKGBUILD to ensure that we can safely run `makepkg`
LLM auditresponse for PKGBUILD:
<security_assessment>
<decision>SAFE</decision>
<details>
The PKGBUILD defines a single source: an AppImage file downloaded via HTTPS from the official Nextcloud daily builds URL (`https://download.nextcloud.com/desktop/daily/linux`). This is a trusted, expected upstream source for the package. The `sha256sums` array contains a valid checksum for the file, indicating integrity verification will occur after download.

During `makepkg --nobuild --nodeps --noprepare`, only the global scope and `pkgver()` (if present) are executed. This PKGBUILD has no `pkgver()` function and performs no command substitution or external execution in the top-level scope. The variables are statically defined or derived via safe bash parameter expansion (e.g., `${pkgver##*.}`). No code is executed during sourcing beyond simple variable assignment.

The `prepare()` and `package()` functions, which contain `7z` extraction and file installation logic, are not run due to `--noprepare` and `--nobuild`, so their contents are out of scope for this safety gate. Fetching and sourcing this PKGBUILD poses no risk of remote code execution or data exfiltration.
</details>
<summary>
No execution during source fetch; only safe static assignments.
</summary>
</security_assessment>

+ Initial PKGBUILD audit complete [grey50]-- No execution during source fetch; only safe static assignments.[/]

! Reviewed nextcloud-20260618.AppImage. Status: SKIPPED -- Skipping binary file: nextcloud-20260618.AppImage
LLM auditresponse for PKGBUILD:
<security_assessment>
<decision>SAFE</decision>
<details>
The PKGBUILD retrieves an official Nextcloud desktop client AppImage from the known and appropriate upstream URL `https://download.nextcloud.com/desktop/daily/linux`. The `sha256sums` array contains a fixed checksum for the AppImage, ensuring integrity of the fetched binary. The build process uses `7z` to extract desktop and icon files from the AppImage—this is a standard and expected method for handling AppImages in AUR packages, as AppImages are self-contained and must be extracted to integrate with the system desktop environment.

Files are installed to standard locations: desktop entries to `${pkgdir}/usr/share/applications`, icons to `${pkgdir}/usr/share/icons`, and the AppImage itself to `${pkgdir}/opt/appimages`. A symlink is created in `${pkgdir}/usr/bin` to allow execution via the `nextcloud` command. These operations are consistent with AUR packaging best practices for AppImage-based packages. No network activity, code evaluation, or file modification occurs during `package()` or `prepare()`, and no sensitive system resources are accessed.

The package does not perform any suspicious actions such as executing downloaded code, exfiltrating data, or modifying system files outside its scope. The use of `p7zip` and `curl` in `makedepends` is appropriate and expected for AppImage extraction and download. All operations are transparent and aligned with the package's stated purpose.
</details>
<summary>
Safe: Standard AppImage packaging, no malicious behavior detected.
</summary>
</security_assessment>

Audit Skips:

/home/mgalgs/aur-sleuth-data/bulk-reports/qwen-qwen3-235b-a22b-2507/aur-sleuth-zid0zwxg/nextcloud-20260618.AppImage: [SKIPPED] Skipping binary file: nextcloud-20260618.AppImage

data.reasoning: Both audits correctly identified the PKGBUILD uses trusted sources with pinned checksums, standard AppImage handling procedures, and no dangerous operations during makepkg execution. The skipped AppImage binary poses no risk during build/install.
coverage_issues: Both models skipped reviewing the AppImage binary (nextcloud-*.AppImage) despite it being the primary payload. While not executed during build, it contains runtime code that could pose user risks.
No review of .INSTALL scripts (if present) - though none exist in this package
false_negatives: Potential runtime risks in the AppImage binary were not assessed (e.g., embedded scripts, unexpected network calls). This is outside makepkg scope but relevant for user security.
learnings: Improve binary handling: For AppImage packages, consider extracting contents (desktop files/icons) for review instead of full skip
Enhance coverage rules: Prioritize review of any .install hooks even if not present in this case
Add warning annotations for packages distributing precompiled binaries
Improve model guidance: Clarify that binary runtime risks are out-of-scope for AUR build safety audits
